description:
You are working as an integration-developer who has to prove that the three stubbed
    API responses already available in the workstation can be transformed into a single,
    well-formed CSV file that downstream BI tooling can ingest.

    1.  Source material (already present on disk)
        • /home/user/api_responses/customers.json  
        • /home/user/api_responses/orders.json  
        • /home/user/api_responses/payments.json  

    2.  Required output artefacts  
        a) A directory:            /home/user/output  
        b) The merged CSV file:    /home/user/output/sales_summary_v1.csv  
        c) A plain-text log file:  /home/user/output/transform.log  

    3.  Transformation logic the CSV must follow  
        • Join customers → orders (on customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id).  
        • Join that result → payments (on orders.order_id = payments.order_id).  
        • From the final joined set, write exactly these seven columns in this order  
          (including the lower-case header row shown here):  

              customer_name,order_id,item,quantity,amount_original_currency,currency,amount_usd

        • amount_usd values  
              IF payments.currency == "CAD"  
                    amount_usd = payments.amount × 0.75  
              ELSE (for “USD”)  
                    amount_usd = payments.amount  

        • Numeric values in the CSV (both amount_original_currency and amount_usd)
          must be rendered with exactly two digits after the decimal point.

        • The rows must be sorted by order_id ascending.

    4.  CSV layout rules that the automated grader will check  
        • File must be UTF-8, Unix line endings (LF).  
        • The header row appears first, followed by ONE row per joined record.  
        • The field delimiter is a literal comma ‘,’ with NO surrounding spaces.  
        • There must be exactly 4 data rows (one for each matching order).  
        • A single trailing newline character MUST be present at the end of the file.

    5.  Logging requirements  
        • Create /home/user/output/transform.log in plain ASCII.  
        • It must contain exactly the four lines shown below—nothing more,
          nothing less, no blank lines:  

              STEP 1: Loaded 3 customers  
              STEP 2: Loaded 4 orders  
              STEP 3: Loaded 4 payments  
              STEP 4: Generated 4 merged rows  

          The lines must appear in that order and end with a UNIX newline (LF).

    6.  Hints (do NOT show actual commands):  
        • jq can be handy for JSON processing.  
        • You may wish to use temporary files in /tmp or pipelines.  
        • Remember to create /home/user/output before writing into it.

    When you finish, the grader will:
        1. Check that /home/user/output/sales_summary_v1.csv exists and is readable.
        2. Validate the file’s header, row count, row ordering, numeric formatting
           and final newline against the spec in section 4.
        3. Verify that /home/user/output/transform.log exists and contains exactly
           the four required lines in exactly the right order.

    No root privileges are available; everything must be done as the normal user.
    Use only tools that come with a typical minimal Debian/Ubuntu installation plus jq.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing files and their contents (already present when the task starts)
    -->
    <preexisting>
        <file path="/home/user/api_responses/customers.json"><![CDATA[
[
  {"customer_id":1,"name":"Alice","country":"US"},
  {"customer_id":2,"name":"Bob","country":"CA"},
  {"customer_id":3,"name":"Charlie","country":"US"}
]
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/api_responses/orders.json"><![CDATA[
[
  {"order_id":101,"customer_id":1,"item":"Keyboard","quantity":2},
  {"order_id":102,"customer_id":1,"item":"Mouse","quantity":1},
  {"order_id":103,"customer_id":2,"item":"Monitor","quantity":1},
  {"order_id":104,"customer_id":3,"item":"Webcam","quantity":3}
]
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/api_responses/payments.json"><![CDATA[
[
  {"payment_id":5001,"order_id":101,"amount":40.00,"currency":"USD"},
  {"payment_id":5002,"order_id":102,"amount":25.00,"currency":"USD"},
  {"payment_id":5003,"order_id":103,"amount":200.00,"currency":"CAD"},
  {"payment_id":5004,"order_id":104,"amount":90.00,"currency":"USD"}
]
]]></file>
    </preexisting>

    <!--
        Expected artefacts after the agent finishes
    -->
    <expected>
        <directory>/home/user/output</directory>

        <file path="/home/user/output/sales_summary_v1.csv"><![CDATA[
customer_name,order_id,item,quantity,amount_original_currency,currency,amount_usd
Alice,101,Keyboard,2,40.00,USD,40.00
Alice,102,Mouse,1,25.00,USD,25.00
Bob,103,Monitor,1,200.00,CAD,150.00
Charlie,104,Webcam,3,90.00,USD,90.00
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/output/transform.log"><![CDATA[
STEP 1: Loaded 3 customers
STEP 2: Loaded 4 orders
STEP 3: Loaded 4 payments
STEP 4: Generated 4 merged rows
]]></file>
    </expected>
